meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve can't collaborate (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't collaborate. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve Can't Collaborate
content.seo_description: Stop losing clients to collaboration friction. Learn to implement SaaS pricing models that solve 'can't collaborate' issues using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To solve collaboration friction, implement role-based access control (RBAC) tied to tiered SaaS pricing. By gating multi-user features behind premium plans, you incentivize team adoption while ensuring infrastructure costs are covered. Use Antigravity to scaffold the auth architecture and Claude Code to automate the deployment of collaborative environment configurations.
content.intro:
The silent killer of any freelance project isn't a bug—it’s the 'can't collaborate' bottleneck. You’ve built a stellar MVP, but your client is stuck in a silo, unable to invite their team or manage permissions. This friction drains your credibility and forces you into endless, unpaid maintenance cycles that kill your profit margins.

The fix isn't just code; it's architecture. By embedding a robust, tiered SaaS pricing model that unlocks collaboration features, you turn a technical limitation into a revenue-generating asset. We’ll use Antigravity to structure the data models and Claude Code to accelerate the implementation, allowing you to ship high-value, multi-tenant systems in a fraction of the time.

steps.title: Define the Multi-Tenant Data Model
steps.description: Establish a robust database schema that separates individual users from organizations, ensuring collaboration is structurally sound from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Prisma schema for a B2B SaaS platform. Include models for Users, Organizations, and Subscriptions. Ensure the schema supports RBAC (Owner, Admin, Member) and links subscriptions to organization-level permissions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file schema.prisma --task 'Migrate and sync database with new RBAC models'

steps.title: Implement Tiered Pricing Logic
steps.description: Create a backend service that checks subscription status before allowing collaborative actions like 'invite user' or 'shared workspaces'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a TypeScript service layer that validates if an organization has the 'Collaborator' feature enabled based on their Stripe subscription tier.

steps.title: Automate Permission Middleware
steps.description: Inject middleware that blocks non-collaborative requests for free-tier users, preventing unauthorized feature access.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Next.js middleware function that intercepts API calls to organization endpoints and returns 403 if the subscription tier lacks collaboration permissions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit middleware.ts --apply 'Implement subscription-based feature gating'

steps.title: Configure Stripe Webhook Handling
steps.description: Ensure your app automatically upgrades or downgrades access rights the moment a client changes their subscription.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Stripe webhook handler that listens for 'customer.subscription.updated' events and updates the organization's 'isCollaborative' flag in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Setup Stripe webhook listener and test with CLI events'

steps.title: Final Deployment and Smoke Test
steps.description: Deploy the changes and use Claude Code to verify that collaboration features are correctly gated according to the pricing model.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a smoke test script that attempts to invite a user as a 'Free' tier organization and verifies the request is rejected.
steps.claude_code_command: claude deploy --production --verify 'Smoke test for collaboration gating'

pro_tips: Always use a 'Grace Period' logic in your middleware to prevent immediate lockout during failed payment retries, which keeps client trust high.
Implement 'Feature Flagging' decoupled from your pricing logic so you can enable beta collaboration features for VIP clients without changing their subscription.
Log every failed collaboration attempt to a dashboard; this data is your strongest sales tool when upselling clients to premium tiers.
Use unique UUIDs for all organization resources; never expose incrementing IDs in your API, as this leaks how many customers you have to competitors.
Automate your invoice generation to include a 'Collaborators Added' line item to justify the cost of the higher tier automatically.
content.next_steps: After implementing this pricing-gated architecture, focus on creating an 'Admin Dashboard' where your clients can self-serve their billing and team management needs.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for free and start engineering for scale. Our community of high-performing AI builders is shipping products that solve real problems and command premium fees. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
